---
title: "Mann–Whitney U Test in R"
author: "Dr Lindokuhle Dlamini"
output: html_document
---

# Mann–Whitney U Test

The **Mann–Whitney U test** is the **non-parametric alternative** to the independent t-test.

It is used when the assumptions of the t-test (such as normality) are violated.

---

# Load Required Packages

```r
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggsignif)
library(car)
```

---

# Check Working Directory

```r
getwd()

setwd("G:/My Drive/Rhodes/2025/Honours 2025/Intro to R Hons 2025")
```

---

# Import Data

```r
Nonparametric_t_test.xlsx <- read_excel("Nonparametric t test.xlsx")

data2025 <- Nonparametric_t_test.xlsx

View(data2025)
```

---

# Inspect Distribution

```r
hist(data2025$`Plant richness`)
```

---

# Test for Normality

```r
shapiro.test(data2025$`Plant richness`)
```

---

# Test Equality of Variance

```r
leveneTest(`Plant richness` ~ Sites, data = data2025)
```

---
## Download Dataset

<a href="data/Nonparametric t test.xlsx" download>Download the dataset used in this lesson</a>

---
# Perform Mann–Whitney U Test

```r
mann_whitney_result <- wilcox.test(`Plant richness` ~ Sites, data = data2025)

print(mann_whitney_result)
```

---
